# Production Packet — Parent-Company Shrinkflation Tier List (carousel)

**Date:** 2026-06-20 · Generated by the `fullcarts-content` operator. Numbers pulled **live** this date.
**Banner (live):** 2,323 events · 906 brands · 1,837 products · 5.4M raw items → say **"2,300+"**.
> ⚠️ `approved-claims.md` §1 still reads "2,200+". Bump to **"2,300+ events across 900+ brands / nearly
> 1,900 products"** on the next refresh.

---

## The one-liner

**Theme:** *"You can't switch your way out."* A handful of corporate parents own the brands you swapped
**to** — and shrank them too. **One company per slide as a "corporate rap sheet"** — a documented, sourced
cut for *every* brand named, **graded S–D by how many of their brands we've caught shrinking** (the
engagement hook: "is your brand's parent an S-tier shrinker?").

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Format** | `CorpRapSheet` — custom composition (`video/src/compositions/CorpRapSheet.tsx`). 4:5, 1080×1350, one slide/frame. |
| **Tier grade** | Per-company stamp (S–D) by `corporate_tree.distinct_brands` — the count of their brands we've caught shrinking. Bands: **S ≥ 30 · A 27–29 · B 20–26 · C 10–19 · D < 10.** Colors S=red A=amber B=blue C=green. |
| **Series** | *Who Owns Your Cart* / Shrinkflation Tier List (accountability + tier-list engagement) |
| **Music lane** | **Lane 6 — Newsjack** (broadcast tension, "worst revealed last"); Lane 1 alt. Stills silent; bed for the Reel cut. |
| **Platforms** | IG carousel (primary) → cross-post auto-advance cut to TikTok/Reels/Shorts; S-tier (Mondelez) slide to X. |
| **Props** | `video/src/props/carousel-parent-company.json` · **Render** `CorpRapSheet`, stills `0..6` |

---

## Database actions (2026-06-20)

**Cadbury Freddo retraction — done, then REVERTED.** The Freddo 122→99 g event
(`published_changes.id = 8b08eb9f…`, ev 72) was briefly retracted, then **restored** at the founder's
follow-up: `is_retracted=false`, `retracted_at=null`, and its 72 claims back to `status='evidence'`
matched to entity `ea606fb4…`. Confirmed live again in `event_evidence_summary`. **Net DB change: none.**
Freddo is back on the Mondelez slide (Toblerone removed).

**Open item — "Mondelez" mis-filed as a brand.** The site is organized by shelf brand/product, not by
ownership, but **57 entities carry the parent name in `brand`** (`Mondelez` 48 · `Mondelez International`
8 · `Mondelēz` 1) — of which **23 are live** on `/brands`. Their real brand (Cadbury, Oreo, Milka, Sour
Patch Kids, Wheat Thins, Toblerone, Ritz, Chips Ahoy, Clif Bar, Good Thins, Prince) is buried in the name.
The `manufacturer` column is also polluted with shareholders ("Capital Group Companies", like the BlackRock
case). **Cleanup approach pending founder sign-off** (re-brand vs merge-into-existing; see chat).

---

## Gate pass (all three)

1. **5 non-negotiables** — ✅ data-driven · interesting (same-owner twist + tier list) · credible · relatable · reaction-evoking.
2. **Approved-claims** — ✅ All 15 cuts are specific, in-DB, non-retracted entries. Ownership claims hand-verified & current (NOT from `corporate_tree`'s shareholder labels). Banner "2,300+". No §3 claims.
3. **Three-bucket evidence** — ✅ Each named brand shows its **own** documented cut **+ its real product photo** (Bucket-1). Per-row `after/before` bars (gap = what they took) so mixed units never cross-compare. No AI charts/photos.
   - **Photos** downloaded to `video/public/parent-company/` so renders work **offline** (sandbox TLS proxy → headless Chrome rejects remote hosts; local static files dodge it). Missing/failed → clean brand-initial monogram.
   - A few are **brand-representative, not the exact SKU** (Mars Bar / Snickers = the bar). Photo says "this brand"; numbers are the evidence.

---

## The slides

**Cover (frame 0):** **YOU CAN'T / *SWITCH* YOUR / WAY OUT** ·
sub: "5 giants own your cart — graded by how many of their brands we caught shrinking. swipe to the S-tier →"

**Frames 1–5 — one company each, ordered ascending to the S-tier finale.** Header = "CORPORATE RAP SHEET" +
company + grade stamp + "{N} of their brands caught shrinking in the FullCarts database — here are 3:";
body = a row per brand (**product photo** · name · before → after · −% · per-row shrink bar).

| Frame | Company | Grade | Brand | Cut | −% | Evidence |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | **Unilever** | **C** · Petty Thief (12) | Dove | 24 → 22 fl oz | 8.3 | 16 |
| | | | Ben & Jerry's | 473 → 430 ml | 9.1 | 2 |
| | | | Lipton | 1.5 → 1.25 L | 16.7 | 5 |
| 2 | **Mars** | **B** · Frequent Flyer (26) | Galaxy | 110 → 100 g | 9.1 | 12 |
| | | | Mars Bar | 53 → 47 g | 11.3 | 2 |
| | | | Snickers | 1.86 → 1.63 oz | 12.4 | 2 |
| 3 | **PepsiCo** | **B** · Frequent Flyer (26) | Doritos | 9.75 → 9.25 oz | 5.1 | 14 |
| | | | Tropicana | 52 → 46 fl oz | 11.5 | 12 |
| | | | Gatorade | 32 → 28 fl oz | 12.5 | 28 |
| 4 | **P&G** | **A** · Repeat Offender (27) | Crest | 4.1 → 3.8 oz | 7.3 | 21 |
| | | | Charmin | 264 → 244 ct | 7.6 | 11 |
| | | | Tide | 254 → 232 oz | 8.7 | 6 |
| 5 | **Mondelez** | **S** · Serial Shrinker (35) | Oreo | 20 → 18.71 oz | 6.5 | 2 |
| | | | Cadbury Freddo | 122 → 99 g | 18.9 | 72 |
| | | | Ritz | 300 → 227 g | 24.3 | 3 |

**CTA (frame 6):** "agree with the *grades?*" · "search any brand — *free* — at fullcarts.org" ·
persona: "Tired dad, sick of getting ripped off — so I built the receipts. 2,300+ documented cuts across 900+ brands. No ads."

> **Evidence depth varies.** Each company leads with a strong anchor — Dove 16, Galaxy 12, Gatorade 28,
> Crest 21, and **Cadbury Freddo 72** (Mondelez's heavyweight, restored). Some secondary cuts are ev 2–5
> (real & sourced, just thinner); sturdier alternates available on request.

---

## Render

```bash
cd video && npm ci   # if node_modules is cold
CHROME=/opt/pw-browsers/chromium_headless_shell-*/chrome-linux/headless_shell  # sandbox local chromium
for f in 0 1 2 3 4 5 6; do
  npx remotion still CorpRapSheet "out/parent-company/slide-0$f.png" --frame=$f \
    --props=src/props/carousel-parent-company.json --browser-executable=$CHROME
done
```
Renders fully offline (photos are local static files). Cross-post Reel: auto-advancing variant + Lane-6 `.mp3` in `video/public/audio/` (vol ~0.22).

---

## Copy for posting

**IG caption:** "I graded the 5 companies behind your cart by how many of their brands we've caught
shrinking. 🛒 The brands you 'switched to' to escape shrinkflation? Same owners — and Mondelez is a
straight-up S-tier serial shrinker. Every number documented + sourced. Agree with the grades? Check any
brand free at fullcarts.org."

**Hashtags:** #shrinkflation #tierlist #groceries #shrinktok #bigfood #mondelez #pepsico #consumer #inflation #fullcarts

**X version:** "I ranked Big Food by how many of their brands we've caught shrinking. Mondelez = S-tier
(35 brands). The stuff you 'switched to' is all owned by the same 5 giants. Receipts: fullcarts.org" + Mondelez (S) slide.

**Cross-post / music:** master Reel uses the **Lane 6 royalty-free bed** (legal on TT/RE/SH/X + ads);
optional native trending sound on the TikTok/IG discovery cut only. Engagement prompt ("agree with the grades?") drives comments.
